vmWizard.title: Add Environment
vmWizard.description: Choose how Orbion reaches the machine , already-local URL or SSH into a remote VM.
vmWizard.reachMethodLabel: How do you reach this machine?
vmWizard.reachMethodLocalDesc: The loop-task daemon is already running and reachable via a URL (e.g. on localhost or a private network).
vmWizard.reachMethodSshDesc: Connect to a remote VM over SSH. Orbion will probe, install services, and pair automatically.
vmWizard.runtimeDescription: Choose the agent used by default for chats in this environment.
vmWizard.localUrlInvalid: Enter a valid HTTP(S) URL (e.g. http://localhost:8845).

vmWizard.sshKeyPassphraseDescription: Encrypted with your OS keychain before storage.
vmWizard.pickServicesDescription: Select which services to install and start on the VM. Services already running will be left untouched.

vmWizard.setAsMainFirstDesc: This is your first environment, so it will be set as the main VM automatically.
vmWizard.securityNote: Make sure you can SSH into the target. We recommend using Tailscale or a private network. Never expose your environments to the public internet.
vmWizard.mainInvalidTarget: Invalid target "{target}". Use user@host format or a known SSH alias.

vmWizard.mainFailedToStartServices: Failed to start services on the VM.

vmWizard.mainInstallOpenCodeFailed: Failed to install opencode on the VM. Check the remote log for details.
vmWizard.mainDaemonPortBusy: Port {port} is already in use by another process on the VM. The wizard will not restart a daemon it didn't start.

vmWizard.mainTunnelAuthFailed: SSH tunnel auth failed. Check your key or password.

infra.title: Infrastructure
infra.description: Ask about your fleet: machine status, clone repos, create issues, manage services.
infra.unreachable: Unable to reach the infrastructure runtime. Check that the main VM is connected.
infra.helpText:
I can help you with fleet operations. Try:
- machine-status: get the health of all VMs
- clone repo: clone a git repository on a VM
- create issue: file a new GitHub issue or ADO work item
- edit issue: update an existing issue's title, body, or labels
- detect-platform: identify a project's hosting platform from git remotes
- list issues / what's labeled X?: view a filtered slice of the backlog as compact cards
- mark these as X: bulk-add a label to all issues from the last list

platformDetect.envIdRequired: environmentId is required for platform detection
platformDetect.noDirectory: No project directory provided , cannot detect platform. Specify a working directory.
platformDetect.platformDetected: Detected platform: **{platform}** (from {count} remote{count, plural, one {} other {s}})
platformDetect.platformUnknown: Could not determine the platform from git remotes. The repository may use an unsupported host.

issues.noPlatformCli: No platform CLI found. Install `gh` (GitHub CLI) or `az` (Azure CLI) to create issues from chat.
issues.ghNotAuth: GitHub CLI is not authenticated. Run `gh auth login` in a terminal first.
issues.azNotAuth: Azure CLI is not authenticated. Run `az login` in a terminal first.
issues.titleRequired: Issue title is required. Start with a short title on the first line.

issues.stackTruncated: Showing {shown} of {total}. Refine your query to narrow results.
issues.stackEmpty: No issues found matching your query.

labels.ghRequiredForLabels: The GitHub CLI (`gh`) is required to add labels. Install and authenticate it first.

labels.setPickupLabel: Pickup label for this project set to `{labels}`.

labels.noPickupLabel: No pickup label configured for this project. Use "set pickup label <name>" to configure one.

editIssue.issueNumberRequired: Issue number is required. Specify which issue to edit (e.g. "edit issue #42").
editIssue.noChanges: No changes specified. Tell me what to change: title, body, or labels.
editIssue.noPlatformCli: No platform CLI found. Install `gh` (GitHub CLI) or `az` (Azure CLI) to edit issues from chat.
editIssue.ghNotAuth: GitHub CLI is not authenticated. Run `gh auth login` in a terminal first.
editIssue.azNotAuth: Azure CLI is not authenticated. Run `az login` in a terminal first.
editIssue.adoLabelsNotSupported: Label operations are not supported for Azure DevOps via the CLI. You can edit the title and description, or manage labels in the web UI.
editIssue.ambiguousReference: I need a specific issue number to edit. Please provide one (e.g. "edit issue #42").

bulkRelabel.preview:

{issueList}

bulkRelabel.noLabelSpecified: Which label should I apply? Try: "mark these as to-refine" or "add label bug to these".
bulkRelabel.noIssuesInContext: No issues in context. List some issues first (e.g. "list issues labeled to-implement"), then say "mark these as to-refine".
bulkRelabel.issueNumbersRequired: Issue numbers are required for bulk relabel.
bulkRelabel.noLabels: At least one label (add or remove) is required for bulk relabel.

diagnosis.title: Diagnosis
diagnosis.summaryConnectionRefused: The target refused the connection , the service may not be running or the port may be wrong.
diagnosis.nextStepConnectionRefused: Check that the target service is running and the port is correct.
diagnosis.summaryNetworkUnreachable: The network is unreachable , the target host cannot be reached from this machine.
diagnosis.nextStepNetworkUnreachable: Verify network connectivity and that the host is online.
diagnosis.summaryTimeout: The command timed out waiting for a response.
diagnosis.nextStepTimeout: Increase the timeout or check if the target is overloaded.
diagnosis.summaryDnsFailure: DNS resolution failed , the hostname could not be resolved.
diagnosis.nextStepDnsFailure: Check DNS configuration and that the hostname is correct.
diagnosis.summaryTlsError: A TLS/SSL error occurred , the certificate may be invalid or expired.
diagnosis.nextStepTlsError: Check the target's TLS certificate and ensure it is valid and trusted.
diagnosis.summaryCommandNotFound: The command was not found , it may not be installed or not in the PATH.
diagnosis.nextStepCommandNotFound: Verify the command is installed and accessible in the PATH on the target machine.
diagnosis.summaryModuleNotFound: A required module or package was not found.
diagnosis.nextStepModuleNotFound: Install the missing dependency (e.g. `npm install` or `pip install`).
diagnosis.summaryPermissionDenied: Permission was denied , the command or file lacks the required access rights.
diagnosis.nextStepPermissionDenied: Check file permissions or run with appropriate privileges.
diagnosis.summaryDependencyMissing: A required dependency is missing from the environment.
diagnosis.nextStepDependencyMissing: Install the missing dependency and retry the command.
diagnosis.summaryCommandError: The command exited with an error.
diagnosis.nextStepCommandError: Check the log output above for error details.
